sections.paragraph:
ἐριθεία, ας, ἡ (W-H. ἐριθία; s. Mlt-H. 339) found before NT times only in Aristot., Polit. 5, 3 p. 1302b, 4; 1303a, 14, where it denotes a self-seeking pursuit of political office by unfair means. Its meaning in our lit. is a matter of conjecture. A derivation fr. ἔρις is not regarded w. favor by recent NT linguistic scholarship and some consider it also unlikely for the sources fr. which Paul possibly derived the lists of vices in 2 Cor 12:20; Gal 5:20, since ἔρις and ἐριθεῖαι are both found in these lists; yet for Paul and his followers, the mng. strife, contentiousness (so Ltzm., MDibelius, JSickenberger) cannot be excluded (cp. Phil 1:17 w. 15 and s. Anecd. Gr. p. 256, 17 ἐρ.= φιλον[ε]ικία). But selfishness, selfish ambition (PAlthaus on Ro 2:8; M-M.) in all cases gives a sense that is just as prob. W. ζῆλος Js 3:14, 16. κατὰ ἐριθείαν Phil 2:3; IPhld 8:2; ἐξ ἐ. Phil 1:17; οἱ ἐξ ἐ. Ro 2:8 (s. Rdm.2 p. 26; 217 n. 4). Pl. disputes or outbreaks of selfishness (B-D-F §142) 2 Cor 12:20; Gal 5:20. KFritzsche, Comm. in Ep. ad Rom. 1836 on 2:8 pp. 143–48; CBruston, RTP 41, 1909, 196–228.—DELG s.v. ἔριθος. M-M. EDNT. TW. Spicq.

W-H. W-H. = BWestcott/FHort—List 1, beg.

Mlt-H. Mlt-H. = JMoulton/WHoward, A Grammar of NT Greek II: Accidence and Word-formation—List 6

NT NT = New Testament

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

  1. fr. = from

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

Ltzm. Ltzm. = HLietzmann, commentator and editor of various works

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

prob. prob. = probable, probably

IPhld IPhld = Ignatius to the Philadelphians—List 1

Rdm. Rdm. = LRadermacher, Neutestamentliche Grammatik—List 6

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Ep.

Ep. = Epistola/Epistula, when applied to letters mostly pseudonymous; various dates—List 5

  1. = epistle
  2. pp. = pages

RTP RTP = Revue de théologie et de philosophie—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

EDNT EDNT = Exegetical Dictionary of the NT—List 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 392
